reason: The preservation PO reduces to proving simple arithmetic implications like “7 <= j ==> 6 <= j” and “j <= 6 ==> 6 <= j”, yet WP ends with “Prove: false”. This indicates the invariant set is inconsistent/non-inductive at the loop back-edge (it can force contradictory bounds on j after the body update), not merely missing helper facts. In particular, the second invariant (j < i ==> (j==6 && i==11)) is too strong to be maintained as an invariant at every iteration; it describes a specific exit state rather than an inductive property.
suspect_text:
loop invariant j >= i ==> (i + j == 11);
loop invariant j < i ==> (j == 6 && i == 11);

raw.suspect_text:
loop invariant j >= i ==> (i + j == 11);
loop invariant j < i ==> (j == 6 && i == 11);
